target: PRED-106 detail_reasoning in data/predictions.json — insert MAGNITUDE NOTE paragraph quantifying the gap between dome's 16 Hz and the Ca²⁺ ICR frequency at Earth ambient B (~38 Hz @ 50 µT). Addresses curmudgeon SEC-PRED-batch-2026-04-29-PRED106 c1 hole H3 (minor).
insertion_point: After the MECHANISM PROBLEM paragraph (which ends with "...does not engage with the thermal-noise objection."), before the PHANTOM CITATION paragraph.
replacement_detail_reasoning:
KERNEL OF TRUTH. There is a real 40-year published literature on weak-EM ion-cyclotron-resonance effects on cellular calcium signaling (Blackman 1985, Liboff 1985, Blanchard & Blackman 1994 parametric resonance, Adey et al. on chick brain Ca²⁺ efflux). 16 Hz is a recognizable frequency in this corpus and is approximately the Ca²⁺ ICR frequency at a magnetic field strength of about 21 µT (2e × 21e-6 / (2π × 40 amu) ≈ 16 Hz). So the claim sits adjacent to a real research tradition rather than being pure fabrication. The kernel: there exists a published bioelectromagnetics literature claiming weak-EM effects on calcium signaling at frequencies in the 10-100 Hz range, and the cyclotron-condition arithmetic at 16 Hz pins out to a physically realisable field strength.

MECHANISM PROBLEM (Kirschvink critique). The standard objection to Liboff-type ICR in biological systems — laid out by Kirschvink and others in the 1990s and not addressed by the dome — is that in aqueous biological media at body temperature, thermal Brownian motion vastly exceeds the Lorentz force on a single Ca²⁺ ion at micro-Tesla field strengths. The kT energy at 310 K is ~26 meV; the Lorentz energy on a Ca²⁺ ion in a 20 µT field is many orders of magnitude smaller. Coherent ICR requires the ion's gyration to survive long enough for resonance to build up, which it cannot in a viscous medium with kT >> Lorentz work per cycle. This is why even the most-cited positive ICR results (Blackman, Adey) have been replication-troubled and why no consensus mechanism exists. The dome's invocation of 'ECM aetheric permeability' to set the resonant condition replaces the standard B-dependent ICR formula with an unspecified parameter and does not engage with the thermal-noise objection.

MAGNITUDE NOTE. The cyclotron arithmetic does pin out (q=2e, m=40 amu → at 16 Hz, B ≈ 20.84 µT), but the field strength this implies is decoupled from the natural ambient case. At Earth's mid-latitude surface field (~50 µT), the Ca²⁺ ICR frequency under f = qB/(2πm) is ~38 Hz, not 16 Hz; even at the equatorial minimum (~25-30 µT) the resonance sits at ~19-23 Hz. The dome's 16 Hz pins to ~21 µT — roughly 40% of Earth's mid-latitude ambient — a value physically realisable only in shielded or weakened-field conditions, not the natural exposure environment of any standard cell-culture setup. This is precisely why the dome has to invoke 'ECM aetheric permeability' to set the resonant condition: at the real ambient field strength, the natural ICR is more than double their claimed frequency, so an aether parameter is being recruited to bridge a 2× discrepancy between the dome's preferred frequency and the standard Lorentz-derived condition under Earth-typical B. The aetheric-permeability invocation is therefore not just hand-waving — it is doing real work, and the work it is doing is hiding a missing factor of two. Why 16 Hz specifically, then, rather than the 38 Hz that Earth's ambient field would actually predict? The published frequency is plausibly motivated by adjacency to dome-internal resonance numerology (close to twice Schumann 7.83 Hz ≈ 15.66 Hz; close to √2 × Tesla 11.78 Hz ≈ 16.66 Hz) rather than by Ca²⁺ biophysics at Earth-ambient B. We surface this as a stylized observation rather than a derivation we attribute to the dome's source code — the dome's automation does not compute the frequency from B-field input — but it strengthens the falsification line: a defender who concedes that 'real ICR effects might exist at the right field' still has to explain why the dome reports the wrong frequency for natural Earth conditions.

PHANTOM CITATION. PRED-106 cites 'WIN-071's Ca²⁺ ICR 16 Hz confirmation' as the empirical basis. The dome's own wins.html (fetched 2026-04-29, 951 lines) lists WIN-001 through WIN-069 — WIN-071 does NOT exist in the published registry. The dome registry shows '69 confirmed' across all surfaces (title, daily_review.json, ai_manifest.json). The cited WIN-071 is therefore either (a) an unpublished internal claim that has not been ingested into the public site, (b) a typo for some other WIN that actually addresses calcium signaling (none of the WIN-012 mag-gravity, WIN-016 aberration, WIN-026 crepuscular ray, WIN-032 New Jerusalem, or WIN-048 CMB Axis-of-Evil entries do), or (c) forward-reference to a planned-but-unwritten claim. In all three cases the prediction's stated empirical basis is not auditable.

PROTOCOL GAPS. 'Published cell biology literature / replication study' is not a falsification specification. There is no specified cell type (HeLa? primary neurons? lymphocytes? all of which behave differently under EM exposure), no specified field amplitude (the ICR condition is B-dependent and the dome's 'aetheric permeability' framing does not pin it down), no exposure duration, no specified Ca²⁺ readout (Fluo-4 fluorescence? aequorin? patch clamp?), no sample size, no randomization, no blinding, and no pre-registered statistical test. The falsification criterion (<5% Ca²⁺ signaling difference from control) is operationally empty without a pre-specified N and α. As written, the prediction can be 'confirmed' by citing any positive paper from the 40-year ICR literature regardless of its replication status.

EFFECT-SIZE NOTE. The >20% effect size is large compared to typical published claims in the Blackman/Adey corpus (effects of 5-15% are typical, often falling within control variability under tighter replication). A reproducible >20% effect from a single field exposure would be a major finding that the bioelectromagnetics community has been searching for unsuccessfully for four decades.

CLASSIFICATION. Genuinely prospective by date (registered 2026-04-04, window closes 2026-04-30 EOM ≈ 1.5 days from this assessment). Not a restatement of any existing WIN in our data (WIN-071 is a phantom). Verdict assigned: silent_ignore — window closed 2026-04-30 EOM; no result was published by the dome by window close, mirroring PRED-105 trajectory. The phantom WIN-071 citation is independently flag-worthy: it suggests internal-consistency drift between the prediction registry and the wins registry. This is one instance of a broader pattern documented in §2 (Self-Contradictions) and the dome's own version release notes — the V13 Finsler coordinate system in §2.9 takes globe distances as input and outputs 'predicted' distances back to those same inputs (a closed loop, not a prediction); WIN-by-WIN numerical agreements often have parameters fitted retrospectively to the empirical target they are then claimed to confirm; and dome version notes (V51.0 → V51.1) repeatedly retune parameters across releases without preserving prior derivations. A prediction that cites a WIN that does not exist is the cleanest visible case of the same drift: the prediction's stated empirical basis is forward-referenced into a registry that hasn't caught up — which is exactly the failure mode you'd expect when prospective claims and confirming evidence are authored by the same agentic loop without an immutable separation between the two.

arithmetic_verification.Earth_ambient_range: 25 µT (geomagnetic equator) to 65 µT (high latitudes); mid-latitude typical ~50 µT
arithmetic_verification.note: All values cross-checked. The 'more than double' framing comes from 38 Hz / 16 Hz ≈ 2.4 (mid-latitude) and 19-23 Hz / 16 Hz ≈ 1.2-1.4 (equatorial floor); 'roughly half' phrasing in the existing TLDR is at the upper bound of latitudes.

holes_addressed: Curmudgeon PRED-batch-2026-04-29-PRED106 c1 H3 (minor): detail_reasoning understates the magnitude problem; add MAGNITUDE NOTE quantifying the gap between published ICR frequency and the applied 16 Hz / 50 µT conditions
ISS-1781 (minor, missing_argument): same as above — the MAGNITUDE NOTE was the specific deliverable
new_evidence_added: Quantitative ICR arithmetic at Earth ambient B (~50 µT → ~38 Hz; ~25-30 µT → ~19-23 Hz)
Numerical pin: 16 Hz → 20.84 µT (≈40% of mid-latitude ambient)
Schumann 2nd harmonic (15.66 Hz) and √2 × Tesla 11.78 Hz (16.66 Hz) as candidate numerology anchors — flagged as stylized observation, not attributed derivation

anticipated_objections.objection: The dome explicitly replaces standard ICR with 'ECM aetheric permeability' — so applying the standard f = qB/(2πm) formula and complaining about a 2× gap is a category error.
anticipated_objections.response: If the standard ICR equation does not apply, then the Liboff/Blackman/Adey corpus (which is the dome's KERNEL OF TRUTH citation) also doesn't transfer — that literature uses standard ICR. The dome cannot have it both ways: either the standard formula applies (and 16 Hz mispredicts Earth-ambient B by a factor of 2.4×), or it doesn't (and the cited empirical literature isn't doing supporting work). The MAGNITUDE NOTE exposes this either/or; it does not assume the standard formula is the dome's chosen model.

anticipated_objections.objection: The 'numerology' framing (Schumann ×2, Tesla ×√2) is unsourced speculation; you said yourselves that the dome's code doesn't compute the frequency. So why include it?
anticipated_objections.response: Stylized observation, explicitly labeled as such in the text. We mark it as a hypothesis about WHY 16 Hz was chosen, not as derivation we attribute to the dome's source. The substantive content (the 2× field-strength gap, the aetheric-permeability bridge) stands without the numerology framing — which is why we close the paragraph with the falsification line on its own grounds.

anticipated_objections.objection: Earth's surface field varies from ~25 µT to ~65 µT; for a defender willing to assume cell experiments at the lower latitude, the gap closes from 2.4× to ~1.2×, and the 'mid-latitude' anchor is arbitrary.
anticipated_objections.response: The note already addresses this — we cite the equatorial floor (19-23 Hz) and the mid-latitude typical (38 Hz) and the high-latitude top (~50 Hz) to make the latitude dependence explicit. Even at the equatorial minimum, the gap is ~20% above 16 Hz and pinned to a still-non-50µT field strength. The 40% figure is for mid-latitude specifically; the broader point is that 16 Hz tracks no natural ambient regime cleanly.

kernel_of_truth: The cyclotron arithmetic at 16 Hz really does pin to a real, physically-realisable field strength (~21 µT) — this is what makes the prediction adjacent to a real research tradition (Blackman/Liboff/Adey 1980s-90s) rather than pure numerology. The dome's choice is not arithmetic-arbitrary; the issue is that the pinned field strength is decoupled from Earth ambient. The MAGNITUDE NOTE preserves this kernel acknowledgement (existing detail_reasoning's KERNEL OF TRUTH paragraph stays intact) while quantifying where the framing breaks.
